title: Abstract Classes and Interfaces
description: Using abstract classes and implementing interfaces.

slides.title: Abstract Classes 📜
slides.content:
# Abstract Classes

An **abstract class** cannot be instantiated. It may contain abstract methods (without implementation).

Use abstract classes to define a contract for subclasses.

* Declare with `abstract` keyword.
* Abstract methods have no body.

quizData.question: Can you create an object of an abstract class?

options.text: Only if it has no abstract methods

quizData.explanation: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly.

slides.title: Abstract Class and Implementation
slides.content: Define an abstract `Shape` and implement it in `Circle`:

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Every class is an interface

In Dart, every class implicitly defines an interface. You can implement a class using `implements` to provide your own implementation of all its members.
